channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: California chemical tank has cracked, causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
item.description: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.
item.content:encoded:
One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Hacky sack is the latest trend, but its increased popularity is causing a shortage
item.description: Numerous purveyors are reporting an abrupt explosion in popularity of the peak '90s toy known as the hacky sack after recent viral social media videos, triggering shortages.
item.content:encoded: Numerous purveyors are reporting an abrupt explosion in popularity of the peak '90s toy known as the hacky sack after recent viral social media videos, triggering shortages.
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Research finds teen summer jobs help reduce crime
item.description: Data shows that summer jobs programs for teenagers have big impacts in reducing crime. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks about it with economist Sara Heller.
item.content:encoded: Data shows that summer jobs programs for teenagers have big impacts in reducing crime. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks about it with economist Sara Heller.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Torn by war, Israelis and Palestinians tie their fortunes together
item.description: At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.
item.content:encoded:
At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.

(Image credit: Dena Yadin)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says
item.description: A man who opened fire Saturday near a White House security checkpoint is dead after being shot by officers who returned fire, the U.S. Secret Service said. It was the third incidence of gunfire in the vicinity of President Donald Trump in the past month.
item.content:encoded:
A man who opened fire Saturday near a White House security checkpoint is dead after being shot by officers who returned fire, the U.S. Secret Service said. It was the third incidence of gunfire in the vicinity of President Donald Trump in the past month.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The fascinating way Milwaukee tenants are fighting one of the city's largest landlords
item.description: In the series "How to Evict Your Landlord," WUWM reporters Sam Woods and Jimmy Gutierrez tell the story of how a group of tenants are working to push out one of the city's largest corporate landlords.
item.content:encoded: In the series "How to Evict Your Landlord," WUWM reporters Sam Woods and Jimmy Gutierrez tell the story of how a group of tenants are working to push out one of the city's largest corporate landlords.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
item.description: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.
item.content:encoded:
Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, his family says
item.description: The two-time NASCAR champion died on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.
item.content:encoded:
The two-time NASCAR champion died on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.

(Image credit: Derik Hamilton)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tennessee judge dismisses federal human smuggling charges against Kilmar Abrego García
item.description: A federal judge in Tennessee cleared Kilmar Abrego Garcia of all criminal charges. His case gained attention when the Trump administration wrongly deported him to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.content:encoded: A federal judge in Tennessee cleared Kilmar Abrego Garcia of all criminal charges. His case gained attention when the Trump administration wrongly deported him to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Congress tackles crises of lowering housing costs and boosting overall housing supply
item.description: America's housing market has two major problems – not enough homes for sale and the ones that are available are too pricey. Congress hopes its latest bill will help with both.
item.content:encoded: America's housing market has two major problems – not enough homes for sale and the ones that are available are too pricey. Congress hopes its latest bill will help with both.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
